answers.value: Request for Temporary Remote Work Arrangement Due to Family Medical Situation
answers.reasoning: Pre-filled value from ground truth form
Pre-filled value from ground truth form
Pre-filled value from ground truth form

answers.value:
Dear Ms. Whitfield,

I am writing to formally request a temporary remote work arrangement due to an unexpected family medical situation that requires my increased presence at home over the next several weeks.

By way of context, my mother, Elena Ramirez (age 71), experienced complications following a minor surgery on 2026-01-15. While she is stable and recovering, her mobility is significantly limited, and she has been advised by her physician not to remain at home alone for extended periods for at least the next 4–6 weeks. As her primary caregiver living closest to her (I am approximately 20 minutes away from her apartment in West Seattle), I need to be available throughout the day to assist with medication management, physical therapy exercises, meal preparation, and general supervision to ensure she does not fall or overexert herself.

I want to emphasize that my request is not to reduce my working hours or responsibilities, but rather to change my primary work location for a defined period so that I can better balance my job duties with these temporary family obligations. I remain fully committed to my role and to meeting all project deadlines and performance expectations.

Proposed Arrangement
--------------------
1. Duration:
   - Start date: 2026-01-27 (next Monday)
   - Initial end date: 2026-03-09 (six weeks), with the understanding that this may be shortened if my mother’s condition improves more quickly than expected.

2. Work Schedule:
   - I propose to maintain my current full-time schedule of 40 hours per week, Monday through Friday, 9:00 a.m. to 5:30 p.m. Pacific Time (with a 30-minute lunch break).
   - I am willing to adjust my hours slightly to better align with team needs, particularly for meetings with colleagues in different time zones. For example, I can start earlier (8:00 a.m.) on Tuesdays and Thursdays to accommodate the product team’s sprint planning and stand-ups.

3. Availability and Communication:
   - I will remain fully reachable during working hours via Microsoft Teams (chat and video), email, and phone.
   - I will keep my status indicators up to date (Available, In a Meeting, etc.) and will respond to messages within 10–15 minutes during core hours, except when I am in scheduled meetings or heads-down development work.
   - For any brief periods when I need to step away to assist my mother (e.g., helping with physical therapy or medication), I will block off my calendar and notify my immediate teammates in our #backend channel.

4. Work Location and Setup:
   - I will primarily work from my home office at:
     4827 38th Ave SW, Unit 3B
     Seattle, WA 98126
   - My mother’s residence, where I will be spending extended time, is:
     1420 Harbor Ave SW, Apt 504
     Seattle, WA 98126
   - I have a dedicated workspace at my home with a desk, ergonomic chair, external monitor, and secure internet connection (fiber, 940 Mbps down / 40 Mbps up). I have been working from home 1–2 days per week over the past year without any connectivity or security issues.
   - When I am at my mother’s apartment, I will use a portable setup (company-issued laptop, privacy screen, and noise-cancelling headset). Her building has stable high-speed internet (cable, 300 Mbps down / 20 Mbps up), and I have already tested VPN connectivity and access to all required internal systems.

5. Job Duties and Impact on Work:
   - My primary responsibilities as a Senior Software Engineer on the Payments & Billing team are:
     • Designing and implementing backend services for the billing pipeline (Node.js/TypeScript, PostgreSQL, and Kafka)
     • Reviewing code and mentoring junior engineers on the team
     • Participating in sprint planning, technical design discussions, and incident response as part of our on-call rotation
   - These responsibilities can be performed effectively from a remote setting, as demonstrated during our previous extended work-from-home periods and my existing hybrid work pattern.
   - Over the last 12 months, I have successfully delivered multiple features while working remotely part of the time, including:
     • The subscription proration logic for mid-cycle plan changes
     • The invoice PDF generation service
     • Improvements to our retry and idempotency handling for payment processor failures
   - I do not anticipate any significant negative impact on my productivity. In fact, when working remotely, I often experience fewer interruptions and can dedicate longer focused blocks of time to complex tasks such as refactoring and technical design.

6. Security and Confidentiality:
   - I understand the importance of maintaining data security and confidentiality, especially given the nature of our billing systems and customer financial data.
   - I will continue to follow all company security policies, including:
     • Using only my company-issued laptop with full-disk encryption enabled
     • Accessing internal systems exclusively via the company VPN
     • Ensuring that my screens are not visible to unauthorized individuals (I use a physical privacy screen and position my desk away from windows and shared spaces)
     • Locking my computer whenever I step away from my desk, even for short periods
   - No company data will be printed or stored physically at my home or my mother’s residence.

7. Coverage and Collaboration Plan:
   - I have already spoken informally with my manager, Daniel Cho (Engineering Manager, Payments & Billing), about this situation, and he is supportive of a temporary remote arrangement as long as HR approves it and we document the expectations clearly.
   - To ensure continuity and transparency, I plan to:
     • Share a weekly priorities list every Monday morning in our team channel, outlining my focus areas for the week
     • Provide a brief end-of-day update in our shared Slack thread summarizing what I completed, any blockers, and next steps
     • Remain available for all recurring meetings, including daily stand-up, sprint planning, backlog grooming, and retrospectives
   - For any in-person meetings that are essential and cannot be conducted remotely, I am willing to come into the office with reasonable advance notice, provided I can arrange alternative coverage for my mother during those times.

8. Contingency Plan:
   - In the event of an unexpected emergency related to my mother’s condition (for example, if she needs to be taken to a follow-up appointment or the hospital), I will immediately notify my manager and the team.
   - If I anticipate being unavailable for more than a brief period during work hours, I will:
     • Update my calendar and Teams status
     • Hand off any urgent tasks (e.g., incident response, code reviews blocking deployments) to an available teammate
     • Make up any lost time later in the day or week, where feasible, to ensure I still meet my full-time hours and deliverables.

Contact Information
-------------------
For reference, my contact details are:

- Mobile phone: (206) 555-4829
- Alternate phone (mother’s landline, for emergencies only): (206) 555-1937
- Work email: michael.ramirez@northbridge-tech.com
- Personal email: mramirez.seattle@gmail.com

I understand that this request may require documentation or additional information, particularly if it intersects with any company policies related to family leave or flexible work arrangements. I am prepared to provide a letter from my mother’s physician confirming her current limitations and the anticipated 4–6 week recovery period, if that would be helpful for your records.

I genuinely appreciate the company’s support for employees who are balancing work responsibilities with caregiving obligations. I value my role here at NorthBridge Technologies and want to ensure that I continue contributing at a high level while also doing what is necessary for my family during this time. My hope is that by formalizing this temporary remote work arrangement, we can set clear expectations and avoid any misunderstandings about my availability or performance.

Please let me know if you need any further details, would like to schedule a brief meeting to discuss this in person or via video call, or if there are specific forms or procedures I should complete as part of this request.

Thank you for your time and consideration.

Sincerely,

Michael J. Ramirez
Senior Software Engineer, Payments & Billing Team
NorthBridge Technologies
(206) 555-4829
michael.ramirez@northbridge-tech.com
